title.en: Trademark Registration in India — Search, Classes, ₹4,500 Filing and the TM-to-® Journey

answerBox.en: A trademark protects your brand name/logo nationally: search the register first (free on the IP India portal), file TM-A in the right class(es) — government fee ₹4,500 per class for individuals/small enterprises (₹9,000 otherwise) — use ™ immediately, answer any examination objection, and if unopposed, registration (®) arrives in roughly 8–18 months. Registration lasts 10 years, renewable forever, and unlocks infringement remedies.

en.label: Examination report reply
en.startsFrom: from the report — miss it, application abandons

en.title: Search first, adopt second
en.summary: The free public search saves rebrands and rejections.
en.detail: Run the **[IP India public search](https://tmrsearch.ipindia.gov.in/tmrpublicsearch/)** (wordmark + phonetic) in your **class(es)** — the Nice classification's 45 classes group goods/services (e.g. 25 clothing, 35 retail/ads, 41 education, 42 software services). Identical/similar marks in your class = choose again now, not after printing packaging. Distinctiveness wins: coined words (invented names) register easily; descriptive terms ("Best Quality Foods") fight objections for years.

en.title: File TM-A — correctly classed and owned
en.detail: File **TM-A** online (IP India e-filing): mark (word/logo/both — a wordmark protects the name across styling), owner (individual/firm/company — with **Udyam/Startup recognition the fee is ₹4,500 per class**; ₹9,000 otherwise), class(es), goods/services description, and **user date** (since-when use, with evidence — or "proposed to be used"). Use **™** from filing day. Multi-class strategy and specification drafting are where professional help pays — mis-classed filings protect the wrong thing.

en.title: Examination, publication, opposition
en.summary: Objections are normal — answer them in 30 days, always.
en.detail: The examiner may object (**Section 9** descriptiveness / **Section 11** similarity) — a reasoned **reply within 30 days** (with evidence of use/distinctiveness) resolves most; hearings follow where needed. Accepted marks publish in the **TM Journal**; third parties get **4 months to oppose** — oppositions are contested mini-proceedings (advocate territory). Unopposed = **registration certificate**, and you graduate from ™ to **®**. Track status on the portal; abandonment for missed deadlines is the #1 avoidable death.

en.title: Use it, renew it, enforce it
en.detail: Registration gives the **exclusive right** to the mark in your classes: infringement suits (injunctions, damages, seizures) plus criminal provisions against counterfeiting; even pre-registration, **passing-off** protects established goodwill. Enforce early — an advocate's **cease-and-desist (₹999)** stops most small copycats. **Renew every 10 years** (₹9,000/class online); non-use for 5+ years invites removal. Register the domain and social handles the same week you file — brand protection is a bundle.

rows.label: Renewal (10-yearly)
rows.note: Online, with surcharge windows if late

en.footnote: Filing yourself is legal and the portal works — but specification drafting, objection replies and oppositions reward experience; budget help where the brand is worth defending.

en: brand name register kaise hota hai

en.q: Can I use ™ without any registration?
en.a: Yes — ™ merely asserts a claim and needs no filing (though it is customarily used from application). ® is reserved for registered marks; using ® without registration is an offence. The real protection ladder: use builds passing-off rights; registration adds statutory exclusivity and easier enforcement.

en.q: How long does registration actually take?
en.a: Smooth applications (no objection/opposition): commonly 8–12 months now. With an examination objection: add months around the reply/hearing. With opposition: 1–3+ years of contest. Your ™ use and priority date are protected from filing throughout — file early, sell meanwhile.

en.q: Which class should a small online business file in?
en.a: The class(es) covering what you actually sell — goods classes for products (e.g. 25 apparel, 30 foods) and often 35 (retail/e-commerce services) alongside; software/apps typically 9 and/or 42. Multi-class filings cost per class — cover today's core + the 2-year roadmap, not all 45.

en.q: Someone is using my registered brand name on Instagram. What do I do?
en.a: Stack the remedies: platform IP-infringement report (registrations get fast takedowns), the ₹999 cease-and-desist, and an infringement suit with interim injunction for persistent offenders — courts grant these readily on a registration certificate. Preserve screenshots and their sales evidence first.

en.q: My application was objected under Section 11 citing a similar mark. Is it over?
en.a: No — Section 11 objections are answered by distinguishing the marks/goods, coexistence evidence, or consent from the cited owner; a large share clear at reply or hearing stage. What kills applications is silence: the 30-day reply window missed = abandoned. Calendar it and respond.

reviewer: Adv. Sneha Iyer, Bar Council of Tamil Nadu & Puducherry, TN/1187/2013
author: WakilBhai Editorial Team
